summary: The test suite covers the three core retention policies (max_age, keep_latest_n, max_total_size), combined policies, the DeletionPlan summary, format_size, and report generation reasonably well. Dry-run vs. execute mode is verified at the report-string level. However, several areas are untested: (1) error handling is completely absent — no tests for invalid/missing JSON fields, bad date strings, missing input file, or malformed CLI arguments, even though the implementation has explicit error paths; (2) CLI argument parsing (--today override, --execute flag, stdin input) is never exercised as a unit test, only indirectly through act; (3) no test for an empty artifact list; (4) only two of the three possible two-way policy combinations are tested and no three-way combination. Design is the strongest dimension: the TDD annotation (RED 1–8), the make_artifact/days_ago fixtures, the TODAY constant, logical class grouping, and independent tests all contribute to good readability and maintainability. Rigor has some bright spots (exact boundary at max_age_days, fewer-artifacts-than-N, multiple deletions to hit size limit, zero reclaimed space) but the total absence of error-scenario tests is a significant gap that would let regressions in the error-handling code paths go undetected.
